feed.title: HealthRankings — Health News
feed.subtitle: Latest US health headlines. Full list: https://healthrankings.co/healthrankings-news.html

entry.title: Public warned after bat tests positive for rabies in southern Utah
entry.summary: A rabid bat found in southern Utah is prompting a warning from public health officials.

entry.title: Evolution Favored Genes Linked to Red Hair – And Vitamin D May Be Why
entry.summary: The DNA blueprint we carry around with us today has been influenced far more by natural selection than we thought, a massive new study reveals, with gene variants behind red hair and pale skin among those selected for survival.

entry.title: 5 Home Exercises That Firm Arm Jiggle Faster Than Gym Sessions After 55
entry.summary: A CPT and fitness director share 5 home moves that rebuild arm strength and reduce jiggle after 55, no gym needed.

entry.title: Scientists Discover Life-Changing Side Effect Of Eating Eggs For Alzheimer Treatment
entry.summary: "Do chocolate mini eggs count?"

entry.title: Man’s GLP-1 Horror Story Leads to Organ Removal
entry.summary: The 44-year-old father is warning others about the dangers of taking weight-loss jabs without medical supervision.

entry.title: Flesh-eating bacteria devour man's arm and leg in just three days
entry.summary: When doctors saw him, his limbs were discolored and crackling.

entry.title: 2 Legionnaires’ disease cases linked to Las Vegas Strip hotel
entry.summary: The Southern Nevada Health District announced on Tuesday that two cases of Legionnaires’ disease were associated with stays at the Wynn Las Vegas, and guests who have stayed at the resort recently are urged to complete a health survey.

entry.title: I'm 38 and I noticed last week that I have been waiting for someone to tell me I'm allowed to rest, and the person I have been waiting for is me, and I have apparently been holding out on myself for about twenty years
entry.summary: Most rest discourse assumes the problem is time management. For a specific kind of high-functioning adult, it isn't. It's authorisation — and the person you've been waiting on to grant it is yourself.

entry.title: Narcissism runs in the family, but not because of parenting
entry.summary: A comprehensive assessment of twins and their families reveals that narcissistic traits are inherited genetically, contradicting the popular belief that parenting styles cause narcissism. Individual experiences outside the home shape the remaining variations …

entry.title: Somerville family hunkers down at home under measles quarantine after exposure at Logan
entry.summary: Neither of the family's children is fully immunized because they are so young.

entry.title: Backyard Chickens Are Spreading Antibiotic-Resistant Salmonella Across the US, CDC Warns
entry.summary: Dozens of people in multiple states have been sickened so far, while several have been hospitalized.

entry.title: A viral over-the-counter meds hack supposedly wipes out anxiety — does it really work?
entry.summary: An over-the-counter one-two punch has gone viral amid claims that it reduces anxiety symptoms, but some doctors are uneasy about the unusual combo.

entry.title: The 9 Best Plant-Based Foods for Protein, According to Nutritionists
entry.summary: These are the best plant-based proteins according to nutritionists. From legumes to seeds, our experts say these nine foods are good sources of protein and share how to add them to your diet.

entry.title: Exclusive | Famed biohacker Gary Brecka reveals his exact routine — including an ‘overlooked performance enhancer’
entry.summary: “I don’t just start my morning, I activate it,” Brecka told The Post.

entry.title: Microplastics turn up in nearly every human brain sample, including healthy tissue
entry.summary: Tiny micro- and nanoplastic fragments seem to be turning up everywhere, including one of the most well-protected parts of the human body—the brain. In a recent study conducted by Chinese researchers, they found microplastics and nanoplastics (MNPs) in nearly …

entry.title: RFK Jr. is holding up $600 million in vaccines for poor countries
entry.summary: The distributor of the shots said it would cost tens of thousands of lives.

entry.title: Lentils vs. Chickpeas: Which Is Better for Fiber and Protein?
entry.summary: These two legumes have incredibly similar nutritional profiles, and they can both be healthy additions to your diet.

entry.title: Celebrating with a Cake Topped with Fish, World’s Oldest Penguin Turns 38
entry.summary: Spneb, who was named after the medication which saved her life, is said to be the oldest Humboldt penguin on the planet.

entry.title: Common occurrence in adulthood could be an early warning sign of brain cancer
entry.summary: Brain cancer is one of the deadliest diseases — and early detection is crucial for better outcomes.

entry.title: Dietitians Say This Is the Number-1 Fruit to Eat for Stable Blood Sugar
entry.summary: Experts share the best fruits to lower blood sugar if you're trying to avoid spikes, plus the simple trick that will help you keep your blood sugar steady.

entry.title: Does Beer Have a 'Surprising Health Benefit'? Here's What The Science Says
entry.summary: Beer could come with a "surprising health benefit", according to a new report from the BBC.

entry.title: This Common Habit Could Signal Serious Health Problems in Later Life
entry.summary: Excessive and early-day napping in older adults correlates with increased mortality risk.

entry.title: Her cancer ‘nightmare’ at age 29 reflects a trend driving UCSF’s new clinic
entry.summary: A surge in early-onset colorectal cancer is prompting UCSF to tailor care for young adult patients navigating work, parenting and treatment.

entry.title: Scientists Discover a Key Difference in Brains That Resist Alzheimer's
entry.summary: The brain changes associated with Alzheimer's usually lead to a severe loss of memory and cognitive abilities, but not always.

entry.title: Eating Your Breakfast at This Specific Time Could Help You Lose More Weight
entry.summary: New research suggests that adjusting the timing of your meals may support more weight loss.

entry.title: Louis "Louie" Pirrotta
entry.summary: Louis “Louie” Robert Pirrotta, 81, passed away on Friday, April 24, 2026, surrounded by his family after a short battle with cancer. Mass of Christian Burial will be held at

entry.title: CDC investigates Salmonella outbreak linked to backyard poultry across 13 states
entry.summary: Data shows one case in New Hampshire and three cases in Maine.

entry.title: 5 things OB/GYNs want you to know about perimenopause
entry.summary: Knowing more about the perimenopause transition can help you advocate for yourself and manage symptoms during this time of hormonal change.

entry.title: MAHA wellness culture is coming for teens. Grown-ups aren’t ready.
entry.summary: A new generation of influencers is changing the face of the wellness movement.

entry.title: Hay fever: Nine tips for coping with pollen and seasonal allergens
entry.summary: From the most effective medications to the most common triggers to avoid, we examine science-backed advice that should reduce your seasonal suffering.

entry.title: Probiotics and Popular Remedies Ineffective for Irritable Bowel Syndrome, Experts Say
entry.summary: TikTok and YouTube are flooded with videos claiming that chamomile tea, abdominal massage, and probiotics are effective for irritable bowel syndrome. While patients easily find and follow these method

entry.title: Efforts to eliminate hepatitis delivers gains but more action needed to meet 2030 targets
entry.summary: Global efforts to combat viral hepatitis are delivering measurable progress in reducing infections and deaths, but the disease remains a major global health challenge, according to a new World Health Organization (WHO)

entry.title: The people who keep their phone face-down on every table aren't being secretive. They learned at some point that being reachable on someone else's schedule was the price of being available, and turning the screen down is the smallest act of sovereignty th
entry.summary: The face-down phone isn't about secrecy. It's a quiet act of autonomy from people who learned, often the hard way, that being constantly reachable carries a real psychological cost — and the wrist turn is the only sovereignty they have left.

entry.title: DOH detected Mpox in Oahu wastewater sample
entry.summary: The Hawaii Department of Health said the sample, collected April 13 at a treatment facility on Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam, tested positive for clade I mpox.

entry.title: DOH: Mpox detected in wastewater sample from Pearl Harbor
entry.summary: The Hawaii Department of Health officials said a wastewater sample from Oahu has tested positive for clade I mpox.

entry.title: How did a carpenter contract hepatitis C at work?
entry.summary: The work comp carrier denied Jack's claim, taking the position that he did not contract the Hepatitis C virus at work. That is when he came to see the experts at Just Work Comp Law.

entry.title: Fluorescent quail embryos could help solve serious birth defects in humans
entry.summary: A fluorescent embryo allows scientists to watch living cells organise themselves in real time, revealing processes previously impossible to observe.

entry.title: UF scientists warn of kissing bugs that can spread parasite in Florida
entry.summary: University of Florida scientists are putting out a BOLO for the winged insects known to spread a deadly, heartbreaking parasite.

entry.title: South Carolina's measles outbreak is over. But more are brewing around the country
entry.summary: The virus infected nearly 1,000 people in the state before the state declared it over. Meanwhile, cases are spreading across many parts of the U.S., with more than 20 outbreaks currently active.

entry.title: Scientists Discovered a Lost Population Near Paris, Solving 5,000-Year-Old Mystery
entry.summary: Ancient DNA from a megalithic tomb near Paris reveals a dramatic population collapse followed by the arrival of entirely new people.

entry.title: I Asked 2 Doctors the Best and Worst Foods to Eat Before Bed, and They Both Had the Exact Same Answer
entry.summary: Plus other tips to get the most restorative sleep.

entry.title: Eating fruits and veg can increase chance of lung cancer according to new study
entry.summary: A new study is raising questions about a possible link between healthy diets and lung cancer risk in younger non-smokers, with researchers pointing to pesticide exposure as a potential factor.

entry.title: AI Turn "Hidden Patterns" Into ADHD Insights
entry.summary: Researchers developed an AI tool that analyzes routine medical records to predict a child's ADHD risk by age 5, years before a typical diagnosis.

entry.title: CDC reports surge in antibiotic-resistant stomach bug
entry.summary: A common stomach bug is becoming harder to treat, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention says, adding to the growing list of infections that can withstand several lines of antibiotics. A CDC report released this month found “extensively drug-res…

entry.title: CDC warns of drug-resistant salmonella infections linked to backyard poultry
entry.summary: Federal health officials are investigating an outbreak of salmonella poisoning linked to backyard poultry that has sickened at least 34 people since February

entry.title: Researchers Finally Solve 50-Year-Old Blood Group Mystery
entry.summary: Scientists have uncovered a hidden layer of genetic regulation that helps explain why individuals with the same blood type can differ so dramatically at the molecular level.

entry.title: Jeff Olson Obituary
entry.summary: Jeffrey John Olson (46), of Suring, WI, passed away on April 25th after a battle with stomach cancer. Born on January 24, 1980, Jeff was the son of...

entry.title: Children may be born with two complex cognitive functions already established, research reveals
entry.summary: A new study is the first to show that two of our most sophisticated cognitive functions, using and understanding language and being able to sense how other people feel, have distinct origins in the brain in young children—matching what we know about the adult…

entry.title: South Carolina’s measles outbreak is over after sickening nearly 1,000 people
entry.summary: South Carolina’s measles outbreak — the worst in the U.S. in more than 35 years — is over, state health officials declared Monday

entry.title: Scientists Discover “Molecular Switch” That Fuels Alzheimer’s Brain Inflammation
entry.summary: A newly identified trigger of brain inflammation could offer a fresh target for slowing Alzheimer’s progression.
